div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Harriman) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 961. Department’s 312, February 16, 4 p.m.See footnote 13, above. In conversation with VyshinskiAndrey Yanuaryevich Vyshinsky, First Deputy People’s Commissar for Foreign Affairs of the Soviet Union. on March 21st I referred to the participation of the Soviet Government on the London Propaganda Committee concerning which I had written to MolotovVyacheslav Mikhailovich Molotov, People’s Commissar for Foreign Affairs of the Soviet Union. on February 26 and inquired whether the Foreign Office had made any decision in the premises. Vyshinski stated that the subject was complicated in substance and in its technical aspects. However, he indicated that a reply to my note would be sent to the Embassy in the near future.
